chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C. P. No. D–2416 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Priority 1. For order on office objections No.1 to 4. 2. For hearing of CMA No.11503/2025. 3. for hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 13.06.2025 Mr. M. Ahmed Hussain, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Nadeem Ahmed Qureshi, Advocate for the respondents a/w Ms. Sadia Ashraf Advocate. Ms. Wajiha Mehdi, DAG..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Learned counsel for the petitioner will be satisfied if this Court will pass a similar order as was passed in C.P. No.D-1943 of 2025 dated 21.05.2025. Learned counsel for the respondents has no objection to such proposition. Order accordingly. The material part of order dated 21.05.2025 is set out below for ease reference: “This Petition was filed by impugning the recovery proceedings initiated by the respondent Department, during the pendency of the appeal before the Commissioner (Appeals) Sindh Revenue Board, Karachi. The respective learned counsel jointly state that it may be just and proper to dispose of this petition with directions for expeditious conclusion of the appeal, while restraining the recovery of the impugned demand in the interim period Accordingly, this petition is disposed of by directing the Commissioner (Appeals) Sindh Revenue Board, Karachi, to decide the pending Appeal expeditiously, preferably within 60 days from the date hereof. However, the respondents shall not take any coercive action against the petitioner, in respect of the impugned demand, till the conclusion of the Appeal.” This petition is disposed of in the above terms alongwith listed application.

The respective learned counsel jointly state that it may be just and proper to dispose of this petition with directions for expeditious conclusion of the appeal, while restraining the recovery of the impugned demand in the interim period Accordingly, this petition is disposed of by directing the Commissioner (Appeals) Sindh Revenue Board, Karachi, to decide the pending Appeal expeditiously, preferably within 60 days from the date hereof. However, the respondents shall not take any coercive action against the petitioner, in respect of the impugned demand, till the conclusion of the Appeal.” This petition is disposed of in the above terms alongwith listed application. HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Asif
